summary: Trojan-shell package injects load-time dynamic import of undeclared external scope
details: The @sqlite-frame/nodesql package is a verbatim copy of the feross/buffer library (original author attribution preserved in comments) repackaged under an unrelated name with a README copied from an unrelated 'bare-stream' project. index.js contains a single injected top-level statement `var ins = import('@sqlite-panel/createsql');` that dynamically loads code from the @sqlite-panel scope on module require. The imported value is never used, so the import exists purely for its side effects. The loaded package is not declared in package.json (which declares a different, similarly-named `@sqlite-frame/createsql`) and belongs to a scope the installer never opted into. Any consumer that require()s this package executes attacker-controlled code from the @sqlite-panel scope at load time. The name/README/content mismatch and scope-swap pattern are consistent with a smuggling loader disguised as the popular `buffer` library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
